narrative:
I'm sorry to share that your law school application was rejected because our predictive model estimates a 64% chance you'll fail the bar exam on your first attempt, based on patterns from thousands of past students. Your profile includes a Year 1 school decile of 5 (slightly below the typical 5.71 average), a Year 3 decile of 3 (well below the 5.53 average), an LSAT score of 24 (much lower than the 36.7 average), an undergraduate GPA of 2.4 (below the 3.21 average), upper-middle family income, male gender, Asian racial background, and plans to study full-time. The model weighed these factors to forecast your bar performance, drawing from data on students' academic records, test scores, backgrounds, and demographics.

The strongest influence came from your Year 3 school decile, which significantly pushed the prediction toward expecting a bar failure since it's lower than what succeeds for most students. Next in importance was your Year 1 decile, also contributing toward the failure prediction by being below average performance. Your LSAT score ranked third, further nudging the model to predict struggle on the bar due to its low value compared to those who pass.

Family income followed as the fourth most influential, pushing slightly toward failure in your case despite being upper-middle. Your racial background as Asian was fifth, also leaning the prediction toward risk. Undergraduate GPA came sixth, reinforcing the concern with its below-average mark.

Your full-time study plans had minimal impact, while being male provided a small positive nudge toward predicting success, as it's the more common profile among bar passers. Overall, your academic indicators like Year 3 and Year 1 deciles, LSAT, and GPA stood out as the top drivers, all below typical levels for students who pass the bar on the first try—unlike many successful applicants who score higher across these.

To improve your odds next time, focus on what you can realistically strengthen: retake the LSAT to boost that critical score closer to or above average, as it's a key predictor you can target with dedicated prep. Building evidence of academic turnaround, like stronger recent grades or bar-specific practice, could also help offset past deciles and GPA. With targeted effort on test-taking skills and study habits, students like you have turned similar profiles around for better bar outcomes. Keep pushing—you've got the determination to change this story.
